mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate on future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the 20-day forward return following rate declines is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a natural rate threshold of 5.5%, tight labor markets signal robust consumer income growth that drives aggregate demand and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.5%, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising retail sales and rising industrial production indicate demand-pull expansion where output growth validates sales growth without immediate supply constraints, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint z-score of RSAFS and INDPRO does not produce positive 20-day returns in walk-forward testing with Rank IC < 0.03, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real earnings expectations and triggers valuation compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns following high inflation readings are positive in more than 55% of observations during tightening cycles, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals deteriorating corporate revenue capacity and weakening economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If negative INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in new privately owned housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and credit availability, which translate into durable goods demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If positive momentum in housing starts is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered quarters, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate confirm labor market absorption without hidden slack, boosting earnings confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of rising payrolls and flat or falling unemployment does not produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of walk-forward instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth above 2% annualized signals aggregate demand outstripping baseline potential, supporting corporate profit expansion and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDP growth above 2% is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations, reject.
